Short title and commencement : ( I ) 'These rules may be called the Central Administrative Tribunal (Financial and Administrative Powers) Rules, 1085. (2) They shall conic into force on the date of their publication ir, the official Gtizet4e. 2. Definitioas : In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires, - (a) "Act" means the Administrative •rilumals Act, 1985 (13 of 1985); (b) "Chairman" means the Chairman of the Tribunal. (c) "Tribunal" means the Central Adminish Tribunal. 3. sittings of an additional ncnch at placc other than the place where it shall ordinarily sit : If at tiny time the Vice-Chairman of any additional Bench is satisfied that circumstances exist which ruder - it neceSs!, ry to have sittings of the said liontit at any place failing within its Pst•ritorial jurisdiction, other' than the place or places rt which it ordinarily sits. he may with •thepreyious consent of the Chairman direct that the Additional Bench shall hold its aitt;ngs at any such 'appropriate place. 4. Powers of Chairman.---The Chairman shall have the same powers as arc conferred on a Depart- ment of the Central• Government in respect of Delet.i.a- . tionof Financial Powers Rules, 1978, General FirS71- cial Rules, 1963. Fundamental and Supplementary Rules, Central Civil Services .(Leave) Rules, 1972, Central Civil Services (inininn Time) Rules, 1970, Central Civil Services (Pension) Rifles, 1972, Central .! Civil Services (c.7on(uct) Rules, 1964, Central . (7iVil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal), Rules, 1965 awl General Provident Fund (Central Services) Rules, 1960. Provided that the exercise of 'these powers slim!l he subject to any procedural or other instructions issued from time to time by the Government; Provided further that in the exercise of powei.; iii respect of items specified in the Schedule, the concur- rence of the Department of Personnel & 'Training in -- 3 of 5 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA the Ministry of Personnel & Training, Administrative Reforms & Public Grievarces and Pension shall bo obtained. INo. A-12018[5185-A SMT, P. V. VALSALA G. .KUTTY, Under Secy. SCHEDULE Delegation of Financial Power Rules Proposals to incur contingent[Miscellancous expenditure of unusual character or where prescribed sealesicorOitions arc required to be relaxed. 2. All proposals to incur contingent expendi- ture exceeding Rs. 20,000 non-recurring in each case and Rs. 5,000 recurring per ar- num in each case not covered by general or special orders. 3. All proposals to incur expenditure on enter-- tairinents including dinners and luncheons not covered by general or special orders of Government. 4. Air-lifting of stores and demurrage charges, including insurance stores except when co- vered by general or special orders. 5. Land acquisition. 6. Renting of Oflice and residential 7. Expenditure orl schemes or projects ( Rule 18 of DEP Rules, 1978). 8. Trading opera( ions. I - • 9. Sale or dismantlement of public buildings. if the book value exceeds Rs, 20.000. 10. Residential telephones to non-entitled • cen.. II, Indents, corVraCts and purchases (Rule 21 of DEP Rules, 197 8)—Proposals in excess of the existing powers. 12. Creation of posts. 13. Re-appropriation. GENERAL FINANCIAL RULES 1. Sanction of permanent advances to new offices. 2 Advances to private firms for supply of stores beyond delegated powers. 3. Declaring an item as countersigned contin- . gent expenditure. : EXTRAORDINARY [PAR r Suc. FUNDAMENTAL RULES AND SUPPLEMI N- TARY RULES I. Declaration as to relative degree of res- ponsibility. of two posts. Declaration of Heads of Departments for the purpose of ERs and SRsIDEP 3. Fixing the pay of a retired Government servant employed in a post carrying a si.inc- tiotted scale of pay, if pay is proposed to be fixed at a stage higher than (he mini- Mum of the scale of the poet or where the pay plus pension and other 1-Or:Anent be- nefits- exceed the last pay drawn. 4. Grarling of premature increments. 5. Relaxing the requirements of a rule in the CCS (Leave) Rules, 1972 under spe- cial circumstances. 6. Deputations abroad, including to the neigh- bouring countries. 7. Permitting a (imminent servant accom- panying, a foreign delegaitioniVIP a; Liai- son Officer, to avail of the same travel or the same arrangement at outstation for boarding and lodging as provided. 8. Allowing non-official other than a Mem- ber of Parliament or State Legislature or a retired High Court or Supreme Court. Judge to travel by ACC or by air.. 9. Extending the joining time admissible under the 1 Rules beyond the Maximum of 30 days. 10. Allowing previous service under a local fund to cowlt as duly in Governmert ser- vice. I. Air Travel by non-entitled officers. 12. Authorising provisional payment to re-em- • ployed pensioner pending fixation of final Pay. 13. Fixation of pay etc. in terms of the powers. delegated in GIMP OM No. F. (6) . 23-E..111[62 dated 22-6-62. 14. Grant of Study Leave. 15. Sanction of honorariurn exceeding Re . 1000 in each case.
